input.prompt:
I have a standard MSTest v2 project and I heard MSTest.Sdk simplifies things.
Can you help me convert my project to use MSTest.Sdk? Show me what the new
project file should look like.

data.content: With MSTest.Sdk, you replace the `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` with `MSTest.Sdk` and remove the three MSTest package references — the SDK bundles them automatically.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to convert from the traditional MSTest v2 setup (with separate `MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` packages) to the newer `MSTest.Sdk` approach.

With MSTest.Sdk, you replace `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` with `MSTest.Sdk` and remove the three MSTest-related package references. The SDK handles all of that automatically.

Let me update the project file.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText: Two tests are failing. The `DataRow` tests have `long` literals (`1L`, `2L`) being passed to an `int` parameter. MSTest.Sdk ships with analyzers that are stricter. Let me check the log to see which tests failed and why.

data.content: The newer MSTest is stricter about `DataRow` type matching — `1L` (Int64) doesn't match `int` (Int32). This is a pre-existing bug that the old version silently accepted. Let me fix it:

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-bc8ea08203c347d5932f5a290aea117e/UserServiceTests.cs", "old_str": "    [DataRow(1L, "Alice")]
    [DataRow(2L, "Bob")]
    public void GetUserName_ReturnsCorrectName(int id, string expectedName)", "new_str": "    [DataRow(1, "Alice")]
    [DataRow(2, "Bob")]
    public void GetUserName_ReturnsCorrectName(int id, string expectedName)"}

     {
         Assert.IsNotNull(expectedName);

All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of the changes:

**`TestProject.csproj`** — switched from `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` to `MSTest.Sdk/3.6.3` and removed the three package references (`MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) plus the `IsPackable` property (the SDK sets it automatically):

```xml
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.6.3">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**`UserServiceTests.cs`** — fixed `DataRow(1L, ...)` → `DataRow(1, ...)` to match the `int` parameter type. The newer MSTest version (bundled with the SDK) enforces strict type matching that the old version silently ignored.
